key_facts: The package is `specify-cli` version `0.5.1.dev0` with Python >=3.11, Typer/Rich CLI dependencies, pydantic-ai/pydantic-graph, and `specify` plus `specify-teams-mcp` console scripts.
`src/specify_cli/__init__.py` is the main CLI surface and registers `sp-teams`, `quick`, `implement`, `testing`, `project-map`, `result`, `hook`, `learning`, and `eval` subapps.
`specify init` resolves either `--integration` or `--ai` through `INTEGRATION_REGISTRY`, applies agent aliases, validates constitution profile and branch numbering, and scaffolds from bundled assets.

`src/specify_cli/project_map_status.py` owns the canonical `.specify/project-map/index/status.json` path, legacy status fallback, canonical map file list, dirty reason normalization, changed-path classification, freshness inspection, and `complete_project_map_refresh` reason `map-build`.

`src/specify_cli/testing_inventory.py` detects test modules from manifests and reports a healthy Python root module plus Rust engine workspace/crate modules when run from source with `PYTHONPATH=src`.
`src/specify_cli/verification.py` provides reusable shell-command validation result summaries for execution/debug flows.
The active installed `specify` command in PATH is stale for this checkout and lacks the current `testing` subcommand; local-source commands must use `PYTHONPATH=src` or the editable environment.
recommended_atlas_updates: Document `src/specify_cli/__init__.py` as the top-level command router and integration/init orchestrator.
Document project-map freshness as a first-class workflow state surface with canonical and legacy status paths.
Document local-source command invocation caveat for development verification.
unknowns: External installed `specify` on PATH may lag the current checkout; atlas should point development commands at source-aware invocation.
